summary.intent: Build a production-ready Electron desktop app for the gossamer project with file-based routing, a new CheckpointTimeline screen, and live-updating sessions/checkpoints UI sourced from V2 database tables.
summary.outcome: Successfully migrated from React Native/Expo to Electron+Vite+React Router, added CheckpointTimeline screen, fixed WebSocket live-update broadcasts to watch CheckpointMetadata table, and wired sessions UI to use CheckpointSessionMetadata for branch data.

repo: The gossamer app is at /Users/stephanostsoucas/gossamer/app; server at /Users/stephanostsoucas/gossamer/src/server.ts
There are two generations of checkpoint tables: old (CheckpointSession) and new V2 (CheckpointMetadata, CheckpointSessionMetadata). The V2 tables should be used exclusively going forward.
The Session model does not have a branch field — branch is stored only on CheckpointSessionMetadata, keyed by sessionId.
The server's 2s WebSocket broadcast poller originally only watched the Event table, missing checkpoint updates entirely.
runIndex() was originally only called after a 30s delay on startup, causing a cold-start gap in checkpoint data.
The git worktree at /tmp/gossamer-checkpoints gets cleared between macOS restarts, causing non-fatal worktree warnings on server startup.
electron-vite outputs to out/ by default (out/main/index.js, out/preload/index.js, out/renderer/), not dist-electron/.
expo-router uses app/ as its default routes directory, which collides with the project root being named app/; fixed via plugin option root: 'routes'.

code.finding: Original WebSocket broadcast poller only tracked max Event.id; needed to also track max CheckpointMetadata.id to broadcast on new checkpoints.
Sessions endpoint needed LEFT JOIN on CheckpointSessionMetadata to resolve branch per sessionId.
RN shim layer: View→div (display:flex, flexDirection:column), Text→div, TouchableOpacity→div[role=button], StyleSheet.create is a passthrough. This pattern made screen migration mechanical.
Had to fully rewrite from RN to semantic HTML because nested Text→div breaks inline text rendering (block-inside-block issue).
Uses MemoryRouter (not BrowserRouter) — correct for Electron where there is no real URL bar.

workflow: electron-vite dev mode sets ELECTRON_RENDERER_URL env var; main process should check this to loadURL vs loadFile.
When restarting a background server with pkill, the killed process emits exit code 144 — this is expected and not an error.
Task notifications with 'failed' status from previous server kills are stale and can be ignored if a subsequent restart succeeded.
Build server before restarting: npm run build in gossamer root, then restart with --repo-dir flag to enable git-based checkpoint indexing.

friction: expo-router naming collision: routes directory defaulted to app/ which shadowed the project root, requiring manual plugin config and directory rename to routes/.
electron-vite default output path (out/) didn't match the initial package.json main field (dist-electron/main.js), causing Electron launch failure.
tsconfig.json was auto-modified by Expo toolchain, stripping manually added include arrays.
WebSocket live updates were silently broken post-migration because the server-side broadcast poller never watched CheckpointMetadata — no error surfaced, just stale UI.
Stale task failure notifications from killed background processes caused confusion about whether restarts succeeded.
open_items: branch field on Session model: currently fetched via JOIN on CheckpointSessionMetadata but not persisted to the Session table itself — could be denormalized for simpler queries.
Git worktree /tmp/gossamer-checkpoints is ephemeral (cleared by macOS between restarts); server needs to re-prune and re-create it on startup — partial fix applied but may need hardening.
CheckpointTimeline screen uses a hash function for branch colors — no formal color palette or legend.
SessionDetail and other screens still use navigation state (state.title) for titles rather than fetching display names from the API.
No Mantine or other web component library has been installed yet — the Electron migration was done to enable this but it hasn't been added.
SessionTree screen was ported but its usefulness relative to the new table-based sessions view hasn't been re-evaluated.
The sessions table shows 'branch' from CheckpointSessionMetadata but the old CheckpointSession table is still in the schema and may accumulate stale data.
